tests.source_program:
define i32 @xor_operand_from_shift_operation(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %shl = shl i32 %a, 2
  %or = or disjoint i32 %shl, %b
  %xor = xor i32 %or, %shl
  ret i32 %xor
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary does not explicitly cover cases where X or Y operands themselves come from other instructions (like shift) rather than being direct function parameters

entry:

  %or = or disjoint i32 %shl, %b
  %xor = xor i32 %or, %shl
  ret i32 %xor
}

tests.source_program:
define i32 @computed_operands_disjoint_or_xor(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %x = add i32 %a, %c
  %y = shl i32 %b, 2
  %or = or disjoint i32 %x, %y
  %xor = xor i32 %or, %x
  ret i32 %xor
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes X and Y as values but doesn't specify they can be results of other computations. Existing tests use function parameters directly as X and Y.

alive2.src:
define i32 @computed_operands_disjoint_or_xor(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %x = add i32 %a, %c
  %y = shl i32 %b, 2
  %or = or disjoint i32 %x, %y
  %xor = xor i32 %or, %x
  ret i32 %xor
}
